name: Otis Johnson
abstract: Otis Johnson (January 13, 1908 – February 28, 1994) was an American jazz trumpeter.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Barefield
about_quotes.evidence: First I had a band at the Nemo Club with me, Don Byas -- small band, like a John Kirby style band with Don Byas on tenor and I played clarinet. And Otis Johnson on trumpet, Pete Jacobs on drums, Clyde Harte on piano and Bass Hill on bass.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: We had Red Island, Otis Johnson, and Hemp Hill, and Louis. It was four trumpets... I played for about two years alto... Then it changed me to tenor... I played until 1940.

about_quotes.speaker: J. C. Higginbotham
about_quotes.evidence: Luis Russell's band. ... Otis Johnson, trumpet player, said, 'I'm not going to give back nothing. I played for that and I'm keeping it.' ... two-thirds of the musicians in New York working then, they kicked back

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: Otis would be playing the first trumpet... they started marking the music, the arranger would start marking the music, 'Henry Allen' or 'Otis Johnson,' 'Charlie Holmes'...
